message: Very low hemoglobin may require urgent medical attention, especially with shortness of breath, chest pain, fainting, or severe weakness.
Very high WBC may require urgent medical evaluation, especially with fever or severe symptoms.
Very low WBC may increase infection risk and should be reviewed urgently, especially if fever is present.
Very low platelets may increase bleeding risk and need urgent medical review, especially with bleeding or bruising.
Extremely high platelets may require urgent medical evaluation.
Very low fasting glucose can be urgent, especially with confusion, fainting, seizure, or inability to eat/drink.
Very low random glucose can be urgent, especially with confusion, fainting, seizure, or inability to eat/drink.
Very high random glucose may require urgent medical advice, especially with vomiting, dehydration, confusion, or rapid breathing.
Very high fasting glucose may require prompt medical evaluation.
Very high HbA1c suggests poor long-term glucose control and should be reviewed by a healthcare professional.
Very low ANC may be urgent, especially if fever is present.
Very high 2-hour glucose may require prompt medical evaluation, especially with symptoms.
Very high fructosamine may suggest poor short-term glucose control and should be reviewed by a healthcare professional.
Very low ANC may be urgent, especially if fever is present.
